TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi beforefieldinit ContainerServiceNetworkProfileKubeProxyConfig extends System.Object implements class System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel`1<class Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerService.Models.ContainerServiceNetworkProfileKubeProxyConfig>, class System.ClientModel.Primitives.IPersistableModel`1<class Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerService.Models.ContainerServiceNetworkProfileKubeProxyConfig>

Docs.summary: Holds configuration customizations for kube-proxy. Any values not defined will use the kube-proxy defaulting behavior. See https://v<version>.docs.kubernetes.io/docs/reference/command-line-tools-reference/kube-proxy/ where <version> is represented by a <major version>-<minor version> string. Kubernetes version 1.23 would be '1-23'.

MemberSignature.Value: public ContainerServiceNetworkProfileKubeProxyConfig ();
.method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor() cil managed
Public Sub New ()
public Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerService.Models.ContainerServiceNetworkProfileKubeProxyConfigIpvsConfig IpvsConfig { get; set; }
.property instance class Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerService.Models.ContainerServiceNetworkProfileKubeProxyConfigIpvsConfig IpvsConfig
Public Property IpvsConfig As ContainerServiceNetworkProfileKubeProxyConfigIpvsConfig
member this.IpvsConfig : Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerService.Models.ContainerServiceNetworkProfileKubeProxyConfigIpvsConfig with get, set

Docs.summary: Holds configuration customizations for IPVS. May only be specified if 'mode' is set to 'IPVS'.

MemberSignature.Value: public bool? IsKubeProxyEnabled { get; set; }
.property instance valuetype System.Nullable`1<bool> IsKubeProxyEnabled
Public Property IsKubeProxyEnabled As Nullable(Of Boolean)
member this.IsKubeProxyEnabled : Nullable<bool> with get, set

Docs.summary: Whether to enable on kube-proxy on the cluster (if no 'kubeProxyConfig' exists, kube-proxy is enabled in AKS by default without these customizations).

Docs.summary: Specify which proxy mode to use ('IPTABLES', 'IPVS' or 'NFTABLES').
